source_intake_record.raw_question_text: At low temperatures, a mixture of ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ and ${ }^{4} \text{He}$ atoms form a liquid which separates into two phases: a concentrated phase (nearly pure ${ }^{3} \text{He}$), and a dilute phase (roughly $6.5 \text{\textperthousand} { }^{3} \text{He}$ for $T \text{\textless}= 0.1 \text{ K}$). The lighter ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ floats on top of the dilute phase, and ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ atoms can cross the phase boundary (see Fig. 2.23). The superfluid ${ }^{4} \text{He}$ has negligible excitation, and the thermodynamics of the dilute phase can be represented as an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of particles with density $n_{d}$ and effective mass $m^{*}\text{(}m^{*}\text{ is larger than }m_{3}\text{, the mass of the bare }{ }^{3} \text{He atom, due to the presence of the liquid }{ }^{4} \text{He, actually }m^{*}=2.4 m_{3}\text{)}$. We can crudely represent the concentrated phase by an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of density $n_{c}$ and particle mass $m_{3}$. Calculate the Fermi energies for the two fluids.

image_dependency: question_text is fully self-contained and does not reference any figure, diagram, or spatial feature
requires_image=false and text_dominant=true indicate the problem is primarily solvable from text
an image asset exists in the registry, but there is no explicit evidence that it is needed to answer the question
multi_step: the solver must identify the degenerate Fermi-gas formula and apply it separately to two phases
the question requires combining phase-specific parameters: concentrated phase uses density n_c and mass m_3, dilute phase uses density n_d and effective mass m*
the task is not mere retrieval because it involves mapping the physical description of each phase to the correct symbolic expression
verifiability: answer_type=set with two explicit symbolic formulas gives a clear target output structure
the expected answer is a standard physics expression that can be checked against the Fermi-energy formula for a spin-1/2 ideal Fermi gas
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true reduce data integrity concerns

decision_reasons: asset registry is complete with question, answer, and image present and no reported issues
question text appears self-contained, so core semantics are recoverable even without relying on the image
high verifiability score and explicit symbolic set answer make the target output checkable
multi-step reasoning is present, giving the sample useful problem-solving value
notation normalization and symbolic equivalence are manageable evaluation risks, not core-semantic failures

metadata.reasoning_chain:
As $n= rac{8 \pi}{3}\left( rac{2 m E_{\mathrm{F}}}{h^{2}}ight)^{3 / 2}$, we have $E_{\mathrm{Fc}}= rac{h^{2}}{2 m_{3}}\left( rac{3 n_{\mathrm{c}}}{8 \pi}ight)^{2 / 3}$, and
\[
E_{\mathrm{Fd}}= rac{h^{2}}{2 m^{*}}\left( rac{3 n_{\mathrm{d}}}{8 \pi}ight)^{2 / 3} .
\]

We can crudely represent the concentrated phase by an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of density $n_c$ and particle mass $m_3$. Calculate the Fermi energies for the two fluids.
